card.intuition: At each node, go left if target < node, right if target > node — the BST invariant makes every operation a binary search on the tree structure.
card.analogy: A library with numbered shelves: to find book 42, check the middle shelf; too high? Go left half; too low? Go right half. Inserting follows the same path until you find an empty slot.
card.time_complexity: O(h) — O(log n) balanced, O(n) skewed (worst case)
when_to_use: Maintaining a dynamic sorted set with O(log n) insert, delete, and search
In-order traversal of a BST yields sorted output — use for sorted iteration
Finding floor/ceiling, predecessor/successor of a value
Base structure for AVL trees and Red-Black trees (balanced BST variants)
gotchas: BST delete has three cases: leaf (just remove), one child (bypass), two children (replace with in-order successor or predecessor)
For the two-children delete case, find the in-order successor (leftmost node in right subtree), copy its value, then delete the successor (which has at most one child)
An unbalanced BST degrades to O(n) on sorted inputs — always consider AVL or Red-Black for production use

exercise.prompt: Complete BST insertion. Recursively find the correct position and attach the new node.
exercise.solution:
root.left  = insert_bst(root.left,  val)
root.right = insert_bst(root.right, val)
hints: If val < root.val, recurse into the left subtree: root.left = insert_bst(root.left, val)
If val >= root.val, recurse into the right subtree: root.right = insert_bst(root.right, val)
exercise.test_code:
class TreeNode:
    def __init__(self,v,l=None,r=None): self.val=v;self.left=l;self.right=r

def inorder(r):
    if not r: return []
    return inorder(r.left)+[r.val]+inorder(r.right)

root = None
for v in [5,3,7,1,4,6,8]:
    root = insert_bst(root, v)
assert inorder(root) == [1,3,4,5,6,7,8], 'In-order must be sorted'
assert search_bst(root, 4).val == 4, 'Found 4'
assert search_bst(root, 9) is None, '9 not in tree'
root = insert_bst(root, 2)
assert inorder(root) == [1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8], 'Insert 2'
print('PASS')
